macro_banner: ⚠ NFP Jun 5 (85K) · Unemp Jun 5 (4.3%) · CPI MoM Jun 10 (+0.5%) · Core CPI Jun 10 (+0.3%) · PPI MoM Jun 11 (+0.8%)

IOT.guidance_note: FY26 revenue guidance of $1.547-$1.555B implies 24-25% growth, representing a deceleration from Q1's 31% growth due to macro uncertainty and tariff-related deal delays
bullets: Q1 ARR reached $1.54B growing 31% YoY constant currency with revenue of $367M up 31% YoY
$100K+ ARR customers grew 35% YoY to 2,638 customers adding 154 net new versus 105 in Q1 prior year, representing 58% of total ARR
Net dollar retention rate achieved target of approximately 115% with 95% of large customers using 2+ products and 66% using 3+ products
Non-GAAP operating margin reached 14% in Q1 versus 2% prior year with record 79% gross margin and 12% free cash flow margin
International contributed 18% of net new ACV tied for highest ever with Europe accelerating sequentially to highest quarterly mix
Expansion deals showed tens of millions in delays post-Liberation Day in April though some closed in May with record pipeline generation in Q1
risks: Elongated sales cycles following Liberation Day in April as customers prioritize spending on tariff-impacted vehicles and equipment creating deal timing uncertainty
Macro uncertainty from tariffs causing customers to delay digitization spending despite continued strong interest and ROI
Near-term timing risk on deals due to broader macroeconomic environment despite strong medium and long-term positioning

HIVE.guidance_note: Targeting 25 exahash by fall 2025 (from 11.5 exahash in June), fully funded; HPC revenue targeting $100M ARR from $20M current run rate with Toronto data center acquisition.
bullets: FY2025 revenue $115.3M with $56.2M adjusted EBITDA and 22% ROIC, highest in sector and double nearest peer at 12%
Hash rate scaling from 11.5 exahash in June 2025 to 25 exahash by fall, targeting $400M+ annualized revenue at $100K Bitcoin and 3% of global network
Mined 1,414 Bitcoin in fiscal year; currently mining 5.5 Bitcoin daily ($600K/day revenue run rate at $200M ARR)
HPC business reached $20M ARR in May 2025, up from $10M in FY2025; acquired 7.2MW Toronto data center to 3.5x HPC footprint and target $100M ARR
Balance sheet holds $23.4M cash plus 610 Bitcoin worth $61M; pledged to buy back 1,300 Bitcoin at $87K to restore HODL above 2,000 Bitcoin
Gross mining margin compressed to $25.1M (22%) from $37.5M (33%) YoY due to April 2024 halving event reducing rewards by 50%
risks: Halving event in April 2024 cut mining rewards in half, pressuring margins from 33% to 22% and reducing Bitcoin production despite higher hash rate
Rising Bitcoin network difficulty and hash rates continue to pressure mining economics and profitability
Toronto data center requires 9-12 month retrofit for liquid cooling before HPC capacity comes online, delaying revenue contribution

commentary.overnight: NFP crushed expectations, printing 172K vs 85K consensus, with March/April revisions adding 93K more jobs. 10-year yields spiked to 4.54% on hawkish Fed fears and stocks sold off hard in a classic 'good news is bad news' reversal. Bitcoin fell to $61,928 at 9:15 AM ET, down $1,754 overnight and now off 14% over the past week as spot ETF outflows hit a record 13-day streak totaling $4.4 billion while institutional money rotates into AI stocks and the SpaceX IPO.
commentary.positions: Your crypto-linked plays got hammered. BTBT down 11.4%, GLXY -11.5%, HIVE -13.7%, IREN -12.1%, MARA -11.2%, and RIOT -10.2% tracked BTC's collapse to the $62K zone. Quantum stocks cratered as Quantinuum's IPO flopped and Broadcom guidance disappointed: IONQ -13.5%, QBTS -13.7%, QUBT -11.0%, RGTI -14.4%. HIMS at $26.19 is now just 8.4% above your stop. SMCI -11.2% and ORCL -9.7% got caught in the broad tech rout. COPP down 10.3% is now at your stop-loss price of $39.39.
commentary.earnings: IOT beat by 30% on June 4 but guidance was lowered and the stock barely reacted. HIVE missed badly on June 2 but CEO tone remained bullish with hash rate scaling from 11.5 to 25 exahash by fall. ORCL reports June 10 with EPS est 1.96 amid noise that Oracle quietly built a $553B backlog, though the stock dropped 9.7% yesterday on sector rotation.
commentary.macro: CPI prints Wednesday June 10 with headline est 4.2% YoY and core 2.9%, followed by PPI Thursday. After today's monster NFP beat, market pricing for any 2026 Fed cut is evaporating and rate-hike odds are climbing. 10-year at 4.54% is compressing multiples across growth names and crypto.
watch_at_open: BTC $62,000 support — break opens $60K then $55K, per technical analysts
COPP at $39.39 — exactly at stop-loss, any further weakness triggers exit
HIMS $24.00 stop — currently $26.19, only 8.4% cushion remaining
IONQ, RGTI, QBTS quantum names — Quantinuum IPO aftermath and profit-taking continuation
10-year yield 4.54% — further spike kills growth/speculative names
risks: Additional quantum sector selling if Quantinuum IPO weakness sparks further rotation out of speculative pure-plays with extreme P/S multiples (IONQ 109x, RGTI 836x, QBTS 791x per recent data)
Bitcoin ETF outflows accelerate past the current 13-day $4.4B streak, breaking $60K support and triggering cascade liquidations across all your crypto-mining positions (BTBT, HIVE, IREN, MARA, RIOT)
